architecture_assumption: Provider runtime activity is projected and consumed via orchestration.domainEvent; no separate providers.event push channel is expected.

records.rationale: Append/project/receipt are non-atomic; retry can duplicate events.
records.title:
Non-atomic event appending can corrupt state on retry. If an error occurs mid-loop (lines 96-102) after some events are persisted but before the receipt is written, the command appears to fail. A retry generates new UUIDs via `crypto.randomUUID()` in the decider, appending duplicate events. Consider wrapping the loop in a transaction or using deterministic event IDs derived from `commandId`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Ingestion worker loop can terminate on unhandled processEvent failure.
records.title: A dispatch error in `processEvent` will terminate the `Effect.forever` loop, permanently halting event ingestion. Consider adding error recovery (e.g., `Effect.catchAll` with logging) around `processEvent` so failures don't kill the fiber.

records.rationale: Upgrade reject writes then destroys socket without defensive error listener.
records.title: Consider attaching a no-op error listener before `socket.write` (e.g., `socket.on('error', () => {})`) to prevent an unhandled `EPIPE`/`ECONNRESET` from crashing the process if the client disconnects mid-handshake.

records.title: Build script runs TypeScript file with bare node

records.rationale: Normalization inconsistency remains, but upstream callsites now mostly canonicalize inputs.
records.title: Inconsistent input normalization across CheckpointStore methods

records.rationale: Braced rename parsing still breaks paths like src/{old => new}/file.ts.
records.title:
Git's braced rename syntax (e.g., `src/{old => new}/file.ts`) isn't handled correctly. The current slice after ` => ` produces invalid paths like `new}/file.ts`. Consider expanding the braces to construct the full destination path.

records.rationale: ENOENT for missing keybindings config still not handled as empty/default.
records.title:
`loadCustomKeybindingsConfig` fails when the config file doesn't exist, which is expected for new users. Consider catching `ENOENT` and returning an empty array instead.

records.rationale: Template-literal interpolation of Symbol still throws.
records.title: Template literal `${cause}` throws if `cause` is a `Symbol`. Consider using `String(cause)` instead.

records.rationale: Revert completion dispatch remains forked; state consistency window remains.
records.title: Forked revert dispatch risks read model inconsistency

records.rationale: Persisted event can outpace in-memory projection on mid-flight failure.
records.title: If `projectionPipeline.projectEvent` fails after `eventStore.append` succeeds, the event is persisted but `readModel` isn't updated, causing desync. Consider updating the in-memory `readModel` immediately after append (before the external projection), so local state stays consistent regardless of downstream failures.

records.rationale: Broad catch may be intentional for liveness; correctness concern is contextual.
records.title: Engine error handler catches all errors including non-invariant ones

records.rationale: Message fallback retention issue is real, but prior FK-violation claim is overstated.
records.title:
The gap-filling fallback logic can retain messages from turns that are about to be deleted, causing foreign key violations. Consider removing the fallback logic entirely, or filtering `fallbackUserMessages` and `fallbackAssistantMessages` to only include messages whose `turnId` is in `retainedTurnIds`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Pending turn-start map is in-memory only and not rebuilt on bootstrap.
records.title:
The in-memory `pendingTurnStartByThreadId` map isn't restored during bootstrap. If the service restarts after processing `thread.turn-start-requested` but before `thread.session-set`, the `userMessageId` and `startedAt` will be lost since bootstrap resumes *after* the committed sequence. Consider persisting this pending state or processing these two events atomically.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Snapshot sequence can under-report due to extra projectors, but replay impact is lower now.
records.title:
`REQUIRED_SNAPSHOT_PROJECTORS` includes `pending-approvals` and `thread-turns`, but `getSnapshot` doesn't query their data. If these projectors lag behind, the returned `snapshotSequence` will be lower than what the included data actually reflects, causing clients to replay already-applied events. Consider filtering `REQUIRED_SNAPSHOT_PROJECTORS` to only include projectors whose data is actually fetched in the snapshot.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: handledTurnStartKeys still grows without pruning.
records.title: Unbounded memory growth in turn start deduplication set

records.rationale: SessionId-only routing can misassociate events under races/rebinds.
records.title: ProviderRuntimeIngestion processes events for wrong thread on race

records.rationale: Duplicate complete dispatch exists; downstream impact often idempotent.
records.title: On `message.completed`, the message ID is added to the set and `thread.message.assistant.complete` is dispatched. On `turn.completed`, the same set is iterated and `thread.message.assistant.complete` is dispatched again for each ID—including already-completed ones. Consider removing message IDs from the set after dispatching on `message.completed`, or filtering out already-completed IDs before the `turn.completed` loop.

records.rationale: Stale-eventId claim no longer applies; eventId is regenerated per event.
records.title: Shared mutable default metadata object causes stale eventId

records.rationale: fish PATH formatting risk still exists in os-jank path recovery.
records.title: Fish shell outputs `$PATH` as space-separated, not colon-separated. Consider checking if the shell is fish and using `string join : $PATH` instead, or validating the result contains colons before assigning.

records.rationale: -ilc shell invocation can pollute captured PATH output.
records.title: Using `-il` flags causes the shell to source profile scripts that may print banners or other text, polluting the captured `PATH`. Consider using `-lc` (login only, non-interactive) to reduce unwanted output.

records.rationale: prepare() still outside managed effect boundary; failure handling risk remains.
records.title: In `executeUnprepared`, preparing outside an effect and not closing the statement can cause uncaught errors and leaks. Suggest wrapping with `Effect.acquireUseRelease`: prepare in acquire (error-safe) and `finalize` in release.

records.rationale: No longer duplicated; checkpoint ref helper now centralized.
records.title: Duplicated checkpoint ref computation across two files

records.rationale: runPromise result still not caught; rejection can surface unhandled.
records.title: Consider adding `.catch(() => {})` after `Effect.runPromise(handleMessage(ws, raw))` to prevent unhandled rejections from crashing the server if `encodeResponse` or setup logic fails.

records.rationale: Same unhandled rejection path remains in WS message handler.

records.rationale: Optimistic local+server dual update is intentional but can temporarily diverge.

records.rationale: file URL decoding still double-decodes in one path.
records.title: `parseFileUrlHref` already decodes the path (line 46), but `safeDecode` is called again here, corrupting filenames containing `%` sequences. Consider skipping the decode when `fileUrlTarget` is non-null.

records.rationale: Scheme regex still misclassifies script.ts:10 as external scheme.
records.title:
`EXTERNAL_SCHEME_PATTERN` matches `script.ts:10` as a scheme because `.ts:` looks like `scheme:`. Consider requiring `://` after the colon, or checking that what follows the colon is not just digits.

records.rationale: Callback cleanup missing, but practical exposure is low in one-shot startup path.
records.title:
`Effect.callback` should return a cleanup function to close the server(s) on fiber interruption. Without it, the `Net.Server` handles keep the process alive and leak the port if the effect is cancelled.

records.rationale: Chunk-by-chunk UTF-8 decode can still corrupt split multibyte characters.
records.title:
Multi-byte UTF-8 characters split across chunks will be corrupted when decoding each chunk separately. Consider accumulating all chunks first, then decoding once, or use `TextDecoder` with `stream: true`.

records.rationale: Parser/encoder mismatch remains, but encoder path currently low-use.
records.title:
The `+` key can be parsed (via trailing `+` handling) but cannot be encoded because `shortcut.key.includes("+")` returns true for the literal `+` key. Consider checking `shortcut.key === "+"` separately and encoding it as `"space"` style (e.g., a special token), or adjusting the condition to allow the single `+` character.

records.rationale: upsertKeybindingRule read-modify-write remains race-prone.
records.title:
`upsertKeybindingRule` has a race condition: concurrent calls read the same file state, then the last write overwrites earlier changes. Consider wrapping the read-modify-write sequence with `Effect.Semaphore` to serialize access.

`truncateDetail` slices to `limit - 1` then appends `"..."` (3 chars), producing strings of length `limit + 2`. Consider slicing to `limit - 3` instead.

`latestMessageIdByTurnKey` is written to but never read, and `clearAssistantMessageIdsForTurn` doesn't clear its entries—only `clearTurnStateForSession` does. Consider removing this map entirely if unused, or clearing it alongside `turnMessageIdsByTurnKey` in `clearAssistantMessageIdsForTurn`.

records.rationale: Decode and SQL errors still collapsed into one persistence error kind.
records.title:
`SqlSchema.findOneOption` can produce both SQL errors and decode errors, but `mapError` wraps all as `PersistenceSqlError`. Consider distinguishing `ParseError` from SQL errors and mapping decode failures to `PersistenceDecodeError` instead.

records.rationale: Unsafe run() result cast remains; runtime risk depends on raw-result consumers.
records.title: `statement.run()` returns a `RunResult` object, not an array. Casting it to `ReadonlyArray<any>` will cause runtime errors when consumers call array methods. Consider wrapping the result (e.g., `[result]`) or returning a properly typed object.

records.rationale: JSON.stringify(cause) may return undefined despite string expectations.
records.title: `JSON.stringify(cause)` returns `undefined` for `undefined`, functions, or symbols, violating the `string` return type. Consider coercing the result to a string (e.g., `String(JSON.stringify(cause))`) or adding a fallback.

records.rationale: ProviderSessionDirectory upsert remains read-merge-write without concurrency control.
records.title:
The read-modify-write pattern (`getBySessionId` → merge → `upsert`) is susceptible to lost updates under concurrent writes. Consider wrapping in a transaction or adding optimistic concurrency control (e.g., version field) if concurrent session updates are expected.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Null-clearing issue is real for providerThreadId; adapterKey part overstated.
records.title:
Using `??` for `providerThreadId` and `adapterKey` makes it impossible to clear these fields by passing `null`, since `null ?? existing` evaluates to `existing`. Consider using explicit `undefined` checks (like `resumeCursor` does) if clearing should be supported.

records.rationale: Data callback may race before processHandle assignment.
records.title:
Race condition: `processHandle` may be `null` when `data` callback fires, since it's assigned after `Bun.spawn` returns. Consider initializing `BunPtyProcess` first, then passing it to the callback to avoid losing initial output.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Still uses write+destroy rather than end() for rejection response.
records.title: Consider using `socket.end(response)` instead of `socket.write(response)` + `socket.destroy()` to ensure the HTTP error response is fully flushed before closing the connection.

records.rationale: Array chunk UTF-8 decode remains vulnerable to split multibyte corruption.
records.title:
When array chunks contain a multi-byte UTF-8 character split across boundaries, decoding each chunk separately produces replacement characters. Consider using `Buffer.concat()` on all chunks before calling `.toString("utf8")`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: No longer duplicated; helper is centralized.
records.title: Duplicate `checkpointRefForThreadTurn` function across files

records.rationale: Bug pattern exists, but current callsites mostly avoid triggering it.
records.title: When `onOpenChange` is provided without `open`, the internal `_open` state never updates because `setOpenProp` takes precedence. Consider calling `_setOpen` when `openProp === undefined`, regardless of whether `setOpenProp` exists.

records.rationale: Resizable object recreation still retriggers effect/storage reads.
records.title:
The `resizable` object is recreated on every render, causing `SidebarRail`'s `useEffect` to repeatedly read localStorage and update the DOM. Consider memoizing the object with `useMemo`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Number(null) -> 0 path still forces min width on initial load.
records.title:
When `localStorage.getItem()` returns `null`, `Number(null)` evaluates to `0`, which passes `Number.isFinite(0)`. This causes the sidebar to clamp to `minWidth` on first load, overriding the `DIFF_INLINE_DEFAULT_WIDTH` CSS clamp. Consider checking for `null` or empty string before parsing, e.g. guard with `storedWidth === null || storedWidth === ''`.
🚀 Reply "fix it for me" or copy this AI Prompt for your agent:

records.rationale: Transport _tag override risk exists but current callsites are constrained.
records.title: Suggestion: don’t spread `params` into `body`; it can override `_tag` and mishandle non-object values. Keep `_tag` separate and nest `params` under a single key (e.g., `data`), or validate `params` is a plain object.

records.rationale: Schema still cannot express null clear for defaultModel patch.
records.title:
`defaultModel` should be `Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(Schema.String))` to allow clearing the value. Currently there's no way to reset it to `null` since omitting means "no change" in patch semantics.
